{{Quote|Despite being the birthplace of a Daritha, Argai has had no golden age. It is largely unchanged from Xer's days, a hard and cold place inhabited by a hard and cold people.|[[Bleys Harand]], ''[[Travels Amid Strange Stars]]''|<ref name="Author's Cut" />}}
'''Argai''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Cronese Mandate/Legends|Cronese Mandate]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]. It was best known for having been the homeworld of the ancient [[Pre-Republic era|pre-Republic]] conqueror [[Xim/Legends|Xim the Despot]], and the first capital of his [[Xim's empire|empire]].

==Description==
Argai was a chilly rocky world with little to see except scattered ruins and the blaze of the [[Indrexu Spiral]] in its night skies. The planet had once been the site of [[Xim/Legends|Xim the Despot's]] grandest [[Xim the Despot's Royal Palace|palace]], but it became little more than scattered stones over the eons.<ref name="Xim Week" />

[[Sah Gosta]] was a settlement on the planet. The Argaians were renowned and cursed since the first days of the Tion Cluster history as hard-bitten traders and daring pilots with a habit of turning to piracy. It was generally believed that they and their planet had changed little since the days of Xim.<ref name="Xim Week" /><ref name="Author's Cut" />

==History==
The planet was the homeworld of the infamous conquerors, Xim and his father [[Xer VIII]].<ref name="Xim Week" />

Argai was one of the original worlds of the [[Kingdom of Cron]]. Xer conquered nearby worlds in the [[Cronese Mandate/Legends|Cronese Mandate]], founding the [[Kingdom of Argai]] circa [[25,150 BBY]]. He later defeated the [[King of Cron]] [[Ferece]], becoming the new King of Cron.<ref name="Xim Week" />

Xim came to power on Argai in [[25,130 BBY]] where he built one of his palaces.<ref name="TEA" /> He made Argai the capital of his empire and the site of the first of his throne worlds. He extended his domain from the [[Radama Void/Legends|Radama Void]] to the borders of [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]], taking the ancient [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] title ''[[Daritha]]''. After Xim's death, his Empire broke into feuding states. Argai was integrated in the [[Jaminere Marches]].<ref name="Xim Week" />

The planet and the Tion Cluster eventually joined the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]]. In the Republic's final years, the Tion Cluster worlds joined the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatists]] and were later members of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="Xim Week" />
